entry.title: Re: Quiz: incorrect number of selections
entry.content: This looks like some bug. Can you reload the course and open up the quiz again? Then check if it allows you to select any answers.

entry.title: Re: Database Vendor Code: 1427 Error code:-2147215357 [CR...
entry.content:
If it works in one but not the other it's likely environmental issue.

Make sure you are using the same Oracle Client on UAT system.

Check permissions, CR fully supports security, try a different user like the Admin account to see if that works.

And can you remove the SDK Tag, it's not the right one to use.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Future SAP consultant here just expressing my gratitude and interest in the digital skills initiative program!  I've used a lot of MRP/ERP systems throughout my career but SAP is by far the one I prefer to work in.  Thanks for all the useful information @David_Chaviano .

I hit a few of the guidelines for the underrepresented candidates for this program:

  • Military veterans - former Intelligence Analyst
  • Single parent - 2 kids
  • Diagnosed with ADD 4 years ago (drastically helped learning to cope with structure and etc)
  • Geographically limited to career opportunities - with shared custody of my kids there are limited options within a valid commute and remote options are very competitive 
  • First generation college students - first one in my immediate family to pursue a college degree.  Looking to supplement the SAP certifications with a degree in Information Systems
  • People looking to switch their careers mid-life - Looking to use the knowledge I've gained in the MFG industry and going through a few implementations to lead me to a career in SAP Consulting.

All in all I've been through ~5 SAP implementations and/or cleanups.  I've worked with cross functional partners to make sure our design would work for all parties involved and worked directly with the consultants and used past SAP setups to come up with a solution that would work for our current needs.

Thanks again!

entry.title: SAP Inside Track PORTO 2023 - My experience as a speaker
entry.content:
Topic : SAP Integration Suite Overview and real world scenarios .

Author: Shaik Azmathulla | SME (Integration Suite | PO) | Integration Black belter

Greetings, Community Cohort!

"I'm thrilled to share that I had the opportunity to present as a speaker at SAP Inside Track Brazil on October 25, 2023, discussing 'SAP BTP Integration Suite overview and real-world scenarios'. This engaging experience took place through an online platform, making it both an outstanding event and a great learning journey.

My session was focused mainly on the key takeaways below.

  • SAP BTP Integration Overview.
  • SAP’s Integration history and investment.
  • Challenges as a customer to accelerate business outcomes with simplified integration.
  • Hybrid integration strategy.
  • Use cases.

SAP BTP Integration Suite: The SAP BTP Integration Suite is the strategic integration service within SAP BTP. It facilitates the integration of business processes that span different departments, organizations, or companies. It acts as a middleware between the systems that can relate to each other. The integration service then arranges for the technical communication between the components and the correct "translation" of data.

SAP Integration Suite Introduction: SAP Integration Suite represents a cutting-edge integration platform as a service (iPaaS) solution. Integration serves as the cornerstone of building an intelligent enterprise, emerging as the primary strategic focus for organizations worldwide. By seamlessly linking customer, product, employee, and brand experiences with business operations in real-time and continuous fashion, organizations can unlock the complete potential of their system landscape. This empowers them to make informed decisions precisely when needed, leveraging the rich insights derived from interconnected data sources and processes.

 

Integration Suite Capabilities:

Cloud Integration: Cloud integration refers to the process of configuring multiple cloud-based systems to connect and work together seamlessly. It involves combining different cloud services and on-premises systems to function as a cohesive unit, allowing data and processes to be shared and managed across various applications and platforms.

API Management: Get access to simple, scalable, and secure digital assets through application programming interfaces (APIs) and consume these.

Open Connectors: Build seamless integrations with over 160 non-SAP applications using prebuilt connectors.

Integration Advisor: Accelerate the development of business-oriented interfaces and mappings, generate runtime artifacts quickly, and significantly reduce efforts.

Trading Partner management: Manage B2B relationships with multiple trading partners through this easy-to-use user interface that helps simplify the B2B communication between trading partners.

Integration Assessment: Define an integration landscape, based on the Integration Solution Advisor Methodology.

Migration Assessment: Assess your existing integration scenarios to migrate them to SAP Integration Suite.

SAP’s Integration history and investment.

SAP (Systems, Applications, and Products in Data Processing) has a long history of evolving its integration capabilities to meet the needs of businesses worldwide. Integration is becoming a key role in business scenarios. It started with XI in 2003 and continuous investment till now and come up with SAP Integration Suite.

Hybrid integration strategy:

SAP’s integration strategy focuses on providing seamless connectivity and interoperability between SAP and non-SAP systems, both on-premises and in the cloud. The strategy emphasizes flexibility, scalability, and simplicity to meet the diverse needs of modern enterprises. Here are the key elements of SAP’s integration strategy.

Integration Principles: 4 key principles are Cloud Integration, API Management, Open Connectors, and Integration Advisor.

Integration Platform: Versatile, enterprise-grade hybrid integration platform – SAP Integration Suite for holistic integration.

Integration Methodology: SAP Integration Solution Advisory methodology – to guide customers to choose the right integration approaches and tools.

Based on the different scenarios, the following table shows the integration tools selected.

Scenarios

Integration tools

Cloud to Cloud

CI / Other capabilities from Integration Suite

Cloud to on premise

CI / Other capabilities from Integration Suite

On premise to Cloud

CI / Other capabilities from Integration Suite

On premise to on premise

Edge Integration cell

Please find the following link for the rest of the information and demos.

SAP BTP Integration Suite overview and real-world scenarios: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QsrTPaQc1W8

Recognition: I cherish the wonderful memories of the event and the certificate I received at the end.

My sincere thanks to #sapcommunity , I am grateful for this great opportunity.

Thankyou so much @MRobalinho for providing this oppurnity . 

Finally, I would like to thank organizers, and sponsors. It won’t be possible to achieve success without a team.

Upon completion, I am eagerly anticipating the SAP Fortaleza 2024 event.

Best Regards,

Azmath.

entry.title: Advanced Variant Configuration_S/4HANA Public cloud_finished goods stock with material variant
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

We have a requirement to view Finished Goods (configurable material with material variant) stock report in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. Please advise whether any Standard API or CDS view is available in SAP Standard to link the material variant to stock (Finished Goods with material variant)

In Stock overview Fiori app, we can only view the stock attached to the sales order but material variant is not linked to stock in SAP S/4HANA public cloud. Please refer to below screenshot. 

Please advise. 

Thank you,

kind regards

Thangaraj Thomas

entry.title: Congratulations to the Winners of ULatinHack 2024 & video recap
entry.content:
¡Anunciamos con orgullo a los ganadores de ULatinHack! 🏆🎉

Felicitamos a todos los equipos por sus increíbles proyectos y dedicación. Los ganadores de esta edición han demostrado una creatividad y talento excepcionales, creando soluciones innovadoras que tendrán un gran impacto en el sector de la salud.

🏅 Primer lugar: BIOHACKERS - Universidad de Los Andes
🥈 Segundo lugar: DigitAlholics - Universidad Peruana de Ciencias Aplicadas
🥉 Tercer lugar: AndesLand - Universidad de Los Andes

Gracias a todos los participantes, mentores y sponsors por hacer de ULatinHack un evento inolvidable. ¡Sigamos creando juntos el futuro! 🚀💡

#ULatinHack #ULatinHack2024 #Innovación #Tecnología #Salud

Para ver el video de recap del evento por favor consultar este post de linkedin 

Para ver las fotos del equipo ganador y de los ponentes en el evento final ver este post de linkedin

Hasta la próxima edición, entonces! 

SAP University Alliances

entry.title: TCS Postings in FB01/FB70
entry.content:
Is it possible to post TCS (TAX COLLECTED AT SOURCE)related entries in FB01/FB70?

Ex-

Customer A/c DR 1000

To Revenue A/c CR 800

To Output GST A/C CR 150

To TCS A/x CR 50

entry.title: SAP trainings _previous work in SAP GUI (AP, CAP) +Tester
entry.content:
I am currently a UAT test analyst looking to improve/develop/enhance my skills in SAP field (maybe as an SAP consultant).

With over 5 years of experience in AP and Project Controlling in SAP GUI, I am currently involved in a transformation project from SAP ERP to SAP HANA until 2027/2028 within my employer, Allianz . During this project, I have gained experience in testing phases and managing business processes within feeders and SAP processes.

Could you recommend suitable training courses to enhance our existing knowledge in Testing and SAP Fiori/Hanna?

I have a budget of approximately 1400 EUR for courses. I am looking for paid courses to make the best use of this budget.

Thank you very much !

entry.title: Re: SAP Manufacturing Execution training
entry.content: Maybe it depends on NC Code setup. I tested in the latest patch of ME 15.5.

entry.title: Plan Data Upload Starter - Directly upload plan data from a local Excel or CSV file via Story
entry.content:
Overview

As part of the SAC 2023 Q3 release, Planning users can now use the native story trigger to upload flat files directly from story. It will allow the end users to mass upload the data directly from story without the need to go through the modeler.

  • Administrators/Process Owners can define a dedicated end-user data upload job from within the modeler and can upload a file to act as the template structure.

  • Story designers will have access to a dedicated 'data import' planning trigger (similar to data actions). Within the widget they will reference the relevant model and data upload job (described above), in addition to other default settings (e.g., target version, automatic publish).

  • At execution time (i.e., on trigger click), end-users will be prompted to select a source file for upload as well as the target version (if made available by the story designer)

Below is a step-by-step guide on how to enable the upload job and add it into a story.

  • Admin Users can create the upload job with the desired upload template which can be shared with the end users.

  • End users can prepare the data in the template format and upload it directly from the story using the Upload Job Starter.

Pre-requisites: This will be supported only for the new model, on non-SAP data centre tenants.

 

Step 1: Create a Upload Job

Modelers/Admin can now see a Upload Jobs Section under the Data Management Tab along with Import and Export Jobs. Click on the Upload Data button present on the right side to upload the template.

 Source Template File.

 

 Enter the upload job name and click on select source file to select the upload template.  (.csv/.xlsx and .txt are supported). Select the delimiter. Click on Create.

 

Click on Set up Upload.Similar to an Import Job, The Modeler can create necessary transformations to align it with Model Master and Transaction data.

Map to Model Dimension and Measures

 

Validate and Click on Finish to Set up the Upload Job.

 

Click create to complete.

 

Step 2: Add the Upload Job Button to the Story.

Open the story you want to add the Upload Job Starter.Upload Starter Job Widget will be added similar to the Data Action widget.

 

Provide the necessary details

  1.  Name and Description.
  2. Select the desired Model.
  3. Select the Upload Job created in Step 1 as highlighted below.
  4. Select remaining settings like 'Use Recommended Planning Area' or Publish Settings after Execution.
  5. Admin can also fix the version or set it as prompt to be selected by end user.
  6. Once completed ,save the story.

 

Step 3: Upload the File.

On opening the story, the end user can view the widget and use it upload the data. Click on 'Sales Plan Upload' Button.

 

End user will be prompted to select the flat file and version. Make the desired selections and click on Run.

 

Once Completed, user will get a message that the job is completed.

 

In case of any rejects , end user can download the rejected data and correct the entries and run the upload again.

 

Upload activity can also be viewed in the Data management Tab

Upload Activity is also visible in the Activity Log to track the updates made by the end users.

Summary

Upload Job Starter can be very useful to upload mass data flat files by the end users without the need of going to the modeler or transformation. It can be a useful addition to plan data uploads along with data entry.

Hope it helps.

entry.title: Re: RAP Framework - Custom Entities GET_EXPANDED_ENTITYSET

entry.title: Re: Is there an API Endpoint to attach a file to a specif...
entry.content: Remember when doing upload via postman, it is important to pass to file as form-data in body, i tried to send this as raw and it didn't work. This note can help as well https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3417551

entry.title: Re: RAP Framework - Custom Entities GET_EXPANDED_ENTITYSET
entry.content: Thanks for the answer! So it seems that the only way to handle this is to retrieve the header data again when fetching the items, right? I can't think of another way to pass data between sessions except for shared memory, which we can't use in Public Cloud I believe.

I followed this     https://github.com/SAP/open-ux-tools/tree/main/packages/deploy-tooling#configuration-with-logging-enabled   but still have the error. Yesterday was the error status code 503, today without changing anything I got status code 403.

 

Have someone any idea? With detailed explanation please.

 

Thank you

Mahmood

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - Get Value by Data Action
entry.content:
Hi friends,

I have a scenario where I have a dummy data row called "Holder," and I want to copy its values to valid data rows. Please see the table below:

Current State     
Dimension 1Dimension 2 Dimension 3 Dimension 4 Dimension 5 Amount (Measure)
      
Holder####150.000
A1B1C1D1E1 
A2B2C2D2E2 
A3B3C3D3E3 
A4B4C4D4E4 
      
      
Desired Outcome    
      
Dimension 1Dimension 2 Dimension 3 Dimension 4 Dimension 5 Amount (Measure)
      
Holder####150.000
A1B1C1D1E1150.000
A2B2C2D2E2150.000
A3B3C3D3E3150.000
A4B4C4D4E4150.000

I've tried various data action coding techniques but haven't achieved the desired outcome. When I use DATA(Dim1=A1)=Resultlookup(Dim1=Holder, Dim2=#), it generates a dataset for every available master data entry. On the other hand, using FOREACH.Booked doesn't work either, as the master data is used in other data rows as well and therefore generates again unvalid data rows

Does anyone have any suggestions?

@N1kh1l

entry.title: Re: SAC - Maintain Time Dimensions: Hierarchy Not Showing
entry.content: Been figuring out time based calculation views for days now as well. This article is really helpful. However, after creating the time-based calculcation view, the view still does not pop up in the SAC as a way to add a time-based dimension to my live data model coming from the same HDI container in SAP HANA. Any hints on how to access the time dimensions in the SAC? (Ultimately, we want to be able to drill down into date hierarchies in a SAC story.)

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: First AI Project AI Core: Execution does not write Log
entry.content:
Hello,

I am following the blog: Quick Start for Your First AI Project Using SAP AI Core | SAP Tutorials

As per the screenshot the logs are visible when the status is "Completed".

 

But I do not see any entries in the log. I am able to see only two status' after I start execution: "Unknown" and "Completed" when the execution is completed. I do not get "Running" status, I have been closely monitoring with consecutive refreshes. 

Log entry is required to verify the result of pipeline execution. Can someone guide?

Many thanks in advance!

entry.title: Add custom field to F0718 - Post General Journal Entries Fiori App
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm trying to add a custom field to the Fiori app F0718 - Post General Journal Entries using UI adaptation/In-app extensibility. We are on S/4HANA 2022 FPS02 Private Cloud.

I've created the custom field in the correct business context Accounting: Coding Block as mentioned in the app documentation

I've also enabled the custom field for the relevant OData of the app in the UIs and Reports section and published the field:

FAC_GL_DOCUMENT_POST_SRV

I can see that the field is published and is available in the backend.

However, when I open the app F0718 - Post General Journal Entries and enter the UI adaptation mode, there's no option to add this field or any custom field for that matter to the items section. I do not get the option to add a field when I click/right-click anywhere on the expanded items section.

Is there something more that needs to be done to be able to add this field to the app UI using UI adaptation for this app? Please guide.

@Jocelyn_Dart 

 

Best Regards,

Saurabh

entry.title: alm-ts-backend vs alm-ts application roles
entry.content:
Hello All!

By now, I think I understand the meaning of the role templates for alm-ts which is for the SAP Cloud Transport Management application itself and from which 3 role templates are node specific (Export/Import/Transport Operator) which we have to create manually as per our nodes and need:

What I am not sure about is the same role structure for application alm-ts-backend: do we really need to manage transport mgmt related authorizations of the backend systems here on the BTP Cockpit? What happens if I do not provide access to those roles only for the ones under alm-ts itself?

Can you please direct me to the proper documentation explaining this? All I checked so far is the SAP Help document which is quite clear about alm-ts roles but I am in the dark with alm-ts-backend application roles. Also to understand what exactly alm-ts-backend mean and what is the deifference between alm-ts and alm-ts-backend applications from TMS perspective?

Setting Up Role Collections | SAP Help Portal

#SAP Cloud Transport Management

entry.title: Re: Partial scrap confirmation in DM, Quantity Confirmati...
entry.content: Hi Oliver , Use "Auto Split Partial SFC Quantity" in NC data entry config . More option detail here : https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/pod-designer/nonconformance-data-entry. Follow Manoel's Blog for stepwise details. Thanks Rahul

entry.title: Re: How to increase report generation size limit in learn...
entry.content: LMS Reports were generally designed for human consumption and actionable next steps. Whereas some reports can be consumed by other system processes after export, when they are usually this big, it is better to decide if Data Service Extracts should be used instead OR splitting up the report with certain parameters, OR delegating it via HRBP or Mgrs in smaller reports if manual/human review is occuring.

entry.title: Re: Consuming a CAP OData service from SAP Mobile Service...
entry.content: thanks Jitendra, I like option 2, but what about the authentication type of this destination? Also, yes sometimes it could be helpful to have a Fiori app not part of a CAP project, for this I could reuse the same destination. When you deploy a CAP service with a Fiori app in the same MTA, it creates a destination service, are you talking about this one or just creating a new one in BTP?

entry.title: Re: SAP Manufacturing Execution training
entry.content: I used the predefined scripts "Nonconformances at Current Operation" and "No Nonconformances at Current Operation" as is, with no manual change to them. Collecting the FALSE value will auto-log the NC Code, and then on SFC Complete button the script will move the SFC along the Failure path. Collecting the TRUE value does not log any NC Code, and hence SFC Complete will follow the path of No NC script. If you still have an issue. you'd rather submit a support ticket to examine your setup closer..

entry.title: Integration AFO with SAP Disclosure Management
entry.content:
Hello guys,

I am integrating AFO with SAP DM to load data from SAP GR. 

I understand that this AFO will be written locally with the datacache (excel file), however it is currently pointing to development. How do I change this connection when I send the transport from development to production?

I found this note that shows how to change it, but would I have to do it manually on each AFO. If I have 300 AFOs in datacache, will I need to manually adjust each one? Is there no option to do it in mass? 

2448743 - How to replace the system of an existing Analysis Office workbook

https://me.sap.com/notes/2448743/E

Would this be different if I used queries? Since it would be pointing to a connection created in SAP DM and then I can change it in mass via change datalink.

Tks,

Felipe B.

SAP Disclosure Management 

@Marc_Kuipers , @Olga_Vasileva , @guido_nachtigall

entry.title: Trying to assign (SAPLFACI)BKPF to a field-symbol
entry.content:
Hi, I have a custom program that has 2 IF ELSE conditions.

IF IDOC DOCUMENT IS POSTED.

ASSIGN ('(SAPLFACI)BKPF') TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<FS_BKPF>). <---This is the existing code and it works just fine.

ELSEIF IDOC DOCUMENT IS PARKED.

ASSIGN ('(SAPLFACI)BKPF') TO <FS_BKPF>. <---Tried to add this but it does not work. I'm not sure why. Is it because the IDOC is parked? But they both have accounting documents.

ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: Landed Cost Functionality
entry.content: I would expect the standard landed cost document to simple Debit Raw Material and Credit Standard Freight. This document is create after the GRPO and should do exactly what you need

entry.title: Re: CAP LLM Plugin – Empowering Developers for rapid Gen AI-CAP App Development
entry.content: When using the function getRageReponse, is it possible to filter textchunk by metadata? Currently the function takes all the textchunk from the entire table, this can be problematic if table contains textchunk that is not related to the user query.

entry.title: Re: S/4 Hana Cloud Public - Configuring "Value Date Rules...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for your question. 

Is this what you're looking for?

Manage Processing Rules for Bank Statements: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/d6fe6526b26f46e59d9d1dd0f7427fe3.html?locale=en-US

How to Create Processing Rules: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/4865ed7d26304aa1b011db7a52131bea.html?locale=en-US

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Welcome
entry.content:
Dear Niladri,

Thanks for your question. You rightly pointed out why Enterprise Integrations are complex. Just to reiterate

Enterprise integrations are inherently complex due to the presence of heterogeneous systems, diverse applications, and various platforms that need to work together seamlessly. Data silos, where information is isolated in different systems, further complicate integration efforts. Additionally, as enterprises grow, scalability issues arise, making it challenging to integrate new applications and services with existing infrastructure. Compliance and security requirements also add layers of complexity, requiring robust solutions to ensure data integrity and protection.

You will learn how Integration Suite solves these challenges holistically in this course:) So stay tuned.

Regards

Shilpa

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - Get Value by Data Action
entry.content:
you need to put your 
DATA(Dim1=A1)=Resultlookup(Dim1=Holder, Dim2=#...)

into an IF statement to limit masterdata combinations

something like

IF RESULTLOOKUP() != NULL THEN

DATA(Dim1=A1)=Resultlookup(Dim1=Holder, Dim2=#...)

ENDIF

this will copy the value only to already existing combinations 

Best regards,

Michal

entry.title: How to Assign the Settlement Profile to a New Production Order Type SAP Cloud.

Hi,

How to Assign the Settlement Profile to a New Production Order Type. SAP Cloud

 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business ByDesign - understand the new Archive Offerings
entry.content:
Hi Paul,

if such BOs have been created already and are part of the productive tenant the customer used so far, they will also work after moving such tenant into archive mode as it's still the same tenant as explained in the blog.

Best

Dirk

entry.title: Re: Cloud Integration- Working with XML Validator
entry.content:
Nice Post, but is it possible (in case of synchronous scenarios) to handle it as errror (in monitoriing) and not to return a 500 Internal Error to the Sender?

We want to see those message as failed in mootirng but return to the sender the XMLValidationResult too. Either i can log it as sucessful and return the Val.Resoult OR hav it as Failed and Sender receives 500: An internal server error occured: The MPL ID for the failed message is : ...

entry.title: Re: Printing in Freight Orders: PPF-action or is BRF+ pos...
entry.content:
Hi Tim, 

Action Profile - /SCMTMS/TOR_PRINT_ROAD would help with printing certain documents.
Configuration of Printing would provide more details.

Is there specific information that you are expecting to be printed from a FO that's not handled with this action profile?

entry.title: Re: SAP shutdown

We would like to inform you that we have a known issue as per the SAP Note 3279943. Can you please refer to it and confirm if this is the same scenario?

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Issue in the Building and Branding the SAP Service and Asset Manager Application Client for Windows
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
I have successfully created the branding client for Windows and installed the client application. However, when I run or open the application, the screen appears white, and I don't see any data in the application. Could anyone please provide some insights or suggestions to resolve this issue? I have attached screenshots below

SAP Service and Asset Manager

entry.title: Solr upgrade issue with version 8.1 to 9.2 in Commerce Cloud 2211.24
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to update solr version in 8.1 to 9.2 (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE_CLOUD_PUBLIC_CLOUD/aa417173fe4a4ba5a473c93eb730a417/b70eefc75c6a45b5aa00de55b8989ee1.html).

I have installed solr-9.2.1.zip with maven command and also set the following property (only for local environments): solrserver.solr.server.version=9.2.0

mvn install:install-file -DgroupId=org.apache.solr -DartifactId=solr -Dversion=9.2.1 -Dpackaging=zip -Dfile=/FILE PATH/solr-9.2.1.zip

While I run ant all getting following error.

C:\Users\gubbalan\CXCOMCL2211_24\hybris\bin\platform\resources\ant\util.xml:142: The following error occurred while executing this line:
C:\Users\gubbalan\CXCOMCL2211_24\hybris\bin\modules\search-and-navigation\solrserver\buildcallbacks.xml:164: The following error occurred while executing this line:
C:\Users\gubbalan\CXCOMCL2211_24\hybris\bin\modules\search-and-navigation\solrserver\buildcallbacks.xml:206: The following error occurred while executing this line:
C:\Users\gubbalan\CXCOMCL2211_24\hybris\bin\modules\search-and-navigation\solrserver\buildcallbacks.xml:208: The following error occurred while executing this line:
C:\Users\gubbalan\CXCOMCL2211_24\hybris\bin\modules\search-and-navigation\solrserver\buildcallbacks.xml:252: The following error occurred while executing this line:
C:\Users\gubbalan\CXCOMCL2211_24\hybris\bin\modules\search-and-navigation\solrserver\buildcallbacks.xml:412: Unable to resolve artifact: Missing:
----------
1) de.hybris.platform.solr:solr-hybris-customizations:zip:9.2.23

Try downloading the file manually from the project website.

Then, install it using the command:
mvn install:install-file -DgroupId=de.hybris.platform.solr -DartifactId=solr-hybris-customizations -Dversion=9.2.23 -Dpackaging=zip -Dfile=/path/to/file

Alternatively, if you host your own repository you can deploy the file there:
mvn deploy:deploy-file -DgroupId=de.hybris.platform.solr -DartifactId=solr-hybris-customizations -Dversion=9.2.23 -Dpackaging=zip -Dfile=/path/to/file -Durl=[url] -DrepositoryId=[id]

Path to dependency:
1) org.apache.maven:super-pom:pom:2.0
2) de.hybris.platform.solr:solr-hybris-customizations:zip:9.2.23

----------
1 required artifact is missing.

for artifact:
org.apache.maven:super-pom:pom:2.0

from the specified remote repositories:
central (https://repo1.maven.org/maven2)

Could you help me, if I am missing any other steps.

entry.title: Re: Item Validation Event Fires Twice (sometimes)
entry.content:
Hi,

I got this behavior when on the form, there was not another field to receive the focus, after validating the value from the edit box. In other words, there was only one editable edit box. On lost focus, it was returning to the edit box.

entry.title: Embedded Analytics Architecture and Integration
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics Architecture and Integration: Part 1

 

SAP S/4 HANA  Embedded Analytics architecture comprises of 4 layers ,namely

  1. Persistency layer
  2. Modeling layer
  3. Access layer
  4. Visualization layer

In this blog I will be emphasizing more on persistency and modeling layer.

This section explains the building blocks that are part of the business reporting architecture and technical architecture. Building blocks such as persistency layer, modeling layer , access layer, and visualization layer are part of the business reporting architecture and building blocks : SAP S/4HANA backend system ,SAP S/4HANA frontend HTML5,and SAP Fiori frontend server.

Business Reporting Architecture

Let’s have a look at the business reporting architecture with the SAP S/4HANA

Embedded analytics and SAP FIORI UI. There are main 4 layers in SAP S/4HANA:

  • Persistency layer :  This layer refers to all the database tables of SAP S/4HANA . HANA is  the   native in memory database used to store  all the application tables in SAP S/4HANA for online analytical processing (OLAP) and online transaction processing(OLTP).
  • Modelling layer: This layer refers to all the ABAP core data services (CDS) views that are built on the top of the database tables. This tables in S/4HANA are exposed as virtual data models(VDMs) . VDM is intended to be interpreted by view browsers and other functionality which is based on the virtual data model.so, these views can be consumed directly by rich client UIs(such as Fiori tile, SAP Business objects )without any additional software layer.

 Before moving to VDM view types and Data Category we need to know about   Annotations . which is very much important in embedded analytics.

Annotation : which defines the technical and semantic attributes of a CDS object. It provides metadata for a filed/view which is understandable by different run time frameworks eg. Analytic Framework. Annotation can be further categorized into

  • View Annotations :For the entire CDS view ,placed in front of the DEFINE VIEW statement.
  • Element Annotations: For elements in the projection list ,placed in front of or after the element.
  • Parameters Annotation: for parameterized CDS view , placed in front of or after the parameter.
  • Extension Annotations: for CDS view extension ,placed in front  of the extend view statement.

 

Some of the important annotations :

@Abapcatalog.sqlViewName:’XXX’ :Auto creates SQL view corresponding to CDS view with the given name ‘XXXX’

@Abapcatalog.preserveKey:true :preserve key true ignores defaults keys from the database table/view and preserves keys defined explicitly in the view. possible values –true /false

@Accesscontrol.authorizationCheck:#check: Tells whether an authorization check should be performed or not.

@EndUserText.label:’xxxx’:It’s a free text which provides info to the end user.Maximum length would be 40 characters.

@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter:true: Defines the  evaluation of filter conditions in path expressions of the CDS  view.

Furthermore few annotations will be discussed under VDM view types and Data category.

VDM view types and Data Category  :

                  

  • Basic View :  Basic view is created on top of the HANA database

@VDM.viewType: #BASIC

  • Composite View :  on top of basic view we will create a composite view. Here the source of the composite view is Basic view. Composite view cannot access directly from the database table.

@VDM.viewType: #COMPOSITE

  • Consumption View :  consumption view is the final view which is created on top of the composite view. This consumption view is accessed by UI Applications and other tools.

         @VDM.viewType: #CONSUMPTION

  Data Category : which is going to define the data based on the business requirement.  Data Category has 3 types

  • Dimension :   otherwise called as basic view which represents the master data  e.x :  Material Master ,customer Master etc

@analytics.dataCategory:#DIMENSION

  • Fact : Represents Transaction data which contains Measures.

Should not be joined with master data views.

@analytics.dataCategory:#FACT

  • Cube: cube is also nothing but a transaction data but it can be mapped with Master data. Also contains measures. consumption views are built on top of the cube.

@analytics.dataCategory:#CUBE

Associations and joins are the two main topics to be known in embedded analytics.

Associations:

Associations are having similar functionality of joins to fetch data from multiple tables based on join condition ,but they are ‘joins on-demand’ which means they will only be triggered when user would access the required data which needs the association of required tables.

Associations are defined with ‘cardinality’.syntax:association[<cardinality>]

  • 0..1
  • 0..n or 0..*
  • 1..0
  • 1..n or 1..*

n should be an integer(non-zero),this basically establishes the relationship between the source and target table.

Here the source table is vbak and the target table is vbap which are associated together with cardinality 1..* which means ,for every one record in vbak with vbeln (on condition filed)there can be a minimum of 1 record and a maximum of  n(*) records in vbap with vbeln (on condition filed).

If we are confused about what kind of association, we should configure in the cds view then we can apply a rule of thumb: ‘always use: association [1]. This will always trigger an outer join and will work in all cases.

Joins:

It is used to join multiple table together to convert data into a meaningful information and represent to business.

Unlike the association ,if we have used 4 tables with join condition in cds view ,all the joins will get executed when we trigger cds view even though business is looking for fields from any two tables.

Supported join types:

  • Inner join
  • Left outer join
  • Right outer join
  • Cross join

 

 

 Note:  Look forward my next blog ,covering on access and   visualization layer.

entry.title: Re: Landed Cost Functionality
entry.content: Hi Felipe,

entry.title: Physical samples per Handling Unit w/ EWM
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I need help in the following scenario:
1) Create PO -> inbound delivery -> Pack HU in EWM an post to inspection stock

2) I want to have the numbers of packed HUs in the No. Containers field in the inspection lot w/ corresponding UoM for sample item determination and sample size determination.

The goal is to have a 1:1 relationship between physical sample and HU so I can block one specific HU when a physical sample is rejected.

I now that there is this Help portal post: Quality Inspection for Handling Units | SAP Help Portal

But this scenario is not working because I use inspection type 1701.

Every help is welcome! Thank you

entry.title: Re: Automated email on PO Addition in SAP B1
entry.content: Thank for this, but still this involves a manual intervention, as the SAP B1 Mailer prompts to add the recipients. What we are looking here is, an email is auto sent, when a PO is added to Saved Distribution List

entry.title: Re: Landed Cost Functionality
entry.content:
Hi Felipe, Thank you for the advice. I have an additional question based of your comment. If I were to make the freight cost on the landed cost document a standard amount and then post the actual AP Invoice with actual cost would that do the following:

1. Allow me to post a JE with a standard freight amount to a standard freight GL account?
2. There will likely be a variance between the A/P invoice and Landed Cost freight at standard. Can I route that variance to a GL account of my choosing?

entry.title: Re: First AI Project AI Core: Execution does not write Log
entry.content: Here is an update: I am finally able to see the logs. Putting it down here, if it may help someone... So after 30 mts of execution(last changed timestamp) I went to my "Workspaces" and selected the resourcegroup thereby setting the connection as the workspace context. After doing that I went to the ML Operations-->Executions and voila! was able to see the logs in the execution ID entry.

entry.content: Does MRS RM remains the backbone of the project and resource management?

Second note:  2861491

Vishalakshmi.

entry.title: Re: Guide: Deploying CAP Application with SQLite Database
entry.content:
From another response in this other thread about the same topic:

 
I had to add the standard sqlite3 dependency in the package.json:
"dependencies": {
"@sap/cds": "^7",
"@sap/xssec": "^3",
"@cap-js/sqlite": "^1.7.3",
"sqlite3": "^5",
"express": "^4"
},

entry.title: Re: Quiz: incorrect number of selections
entry.content:
Hi @kaush_1 , yes, I've tried it multiple times and different dates, refreshing, reloading, restarting 🙂

I'm able to select the answers, but that error message is always there regardless; in addition, the submit answers button is always grayed out (inactive).

entry.title: Material not valid for plant in Manage material coverage app
entry.content:
Hello,

I am currently facing the problem that when I want to check one specific material in the App Manage Material coverage, it says that the Material is not valid for that plant. When I go into Stocks/ Req. List I can see it for that plant correctly tho. I tried checking any differences in material master for this problematic material vs other materials that show for that plant but I couldnt find any. 

The exact error message it gives is this: Plant DE02 is not valid for material 9000037. The system has therefore changed the plant to DE01.

I already checked AoR so its not because of that. Anybody faced a similar problem?

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Olá Comunidade,

Sou do Sul do Brasil, estou animada por fazer parte da comunidade, adoro compartilhar experiências e fazer novas descobertas.

entry.title: Re: Feedback gathering for GACE - person model for Goals
entry.content:
Hi @VanessaLi ,

Yes, this is a request we get from a lot of customers. Not only for GA/CE though; also for re-hires with new employment.

There are more reasons to want to have performance forms and goal plans on person level then on assignment level (same for Succession, Internal Recruitment, Compensation).

  • For global assignments you the home manager usually remains the end-responsible for the employee; you need the input from the host manager, but the home manager make the final decisions. Also as the global assignment is temporary so the home manager needs to approve the final performance rating as it can have financial consequences (bonus and/or salary increases) which goed from their budget.
  • For concurrent employment there can be a bit more discussion if the performance plans and performance goals should be on person or assignment level. Development goals always relate to the person and not the assignment. Some companies want to run the performance forms, performance goal plans and compensation cycles per employment and some want to combine them into one for the person.
  • For re-hires you want to be able to access the historical performance information without hassle (unless the performance forms are purged)

entry.title: Re: Automated email on PO Addition in SAP B1
entry.content:
The system will use email address automatically by priority as following:

1. email from contact person in PO

2. email from BP master data if there is no contact person of PO

If it still doesn't meet your requirement, you need to develop an addon for the specific case.

entry.title: Re: XSD Validation for DMEEX
entry.content:
Dear Richard,

Thank you for your blog post. Unfortunately, there is no error message in transaction FIBLFFP when the payment file is not valid. If you generate the payment file again in transaction FBPM, you see the error message in the log. But our end users in financial accounting don't use FBPM.

The expectation (or wish) is, that a failed XSD validation causes an abortion of the DME generation and a visible error message in transaction FIBLFFP and F110.

Facing this limitations, this XSD validation in DMEEX is not suitable for us.

Regards

Daniel

entry.title: Re: Multiple FU's are not created for the sales order
entry.content:
Hi Venkatesh, 

Since the dates (assuming delivery dates) are different for both materials, the created FU is probably created with multiple stages.

Does the FU have a stage from SHP1 to SHP2 as well?

Best Regards, 
Keerthana

entry.title: Re: Connect SAP Web Intelligence to SAP Datasphere analytic models using OAuth 2.0
entry.content:
Has anyone had the issues described in note 3453821 and overcome them successfully? 

After upgrading our environment to 4.3 SP4 Patch 5 we're trying to connect to our Datasphere tenant and getting an "<STS> An unexpected error has occurred (FWM 02004)</STS>" error. Can't seem to find a way forward there, and I don't see many other people talking about the same problem.

entry.title: Re: SAC Script needed to retrive the selected values of a...
entry.content:
Hmm,

for which event  to get the "sel" value also of  the initial call of the query input variable ZHIERNODEMULTI?
And how to read than out  "sel"  ...?

You assistance is appreciated.

Best Martin

entry.title: S/4HANA technical replacement for FK01 BDC Create Vendor with reference
entry.content:
Dear Community,

we are currently in a transition to S/4Hana 2023 FPS1.
I need to convert some programs and run into a little tool that calls FK01 to create a new vendor in another company code based on the same vendor from another company code via bdc.

What I found in the SAP Note that this can be done with the Transaction "BP" and then  “Create from Reference” (2579816).
However, I'm searching for a more technical ABAP based approach to replace the old BDC call of FK01 ideally by keeping (nearly) the same parameters.
I checked BAPI’s and other possible interfaces, but could not found a cunning replacement (e.g. checked Note 2484299)

Does someone solve this problem already?
Many thanks in advance.

KR
Markus

entry.title: Re: Overcoming Challenges in Staffing Customer Projects with SAP Project and Resource Management
entry.content:
Hi Suhasku, 

SAP Project and Resource Management covers a similar process as MRS RM, but it is a completely own solution built on BTP. For now SAP Project and Resource Management is only targeted towards staffing of human resource demands in projects. As you may know MRS can also plan and schedule other type of resources .

Best regards Renato

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Meetup Kolkata
entry.content: Interested to join

entry.title: Re: Sloc to sloc against the bin to bin transfer
entry.content: You want to change the destination bin during TO creation or confirmation?

entry.title: Re: SAP Sustainability Footprint Management: Q4-23 Updates & Highlights
entry.content:
Thanks @NicoWottke  for sharing this update

Under freight integration we can see the Sankey chart also includes transport footprints as well as footprints of each supplier. is it also possible to see this integrated footprint view per supplier, so we can compare the summation scope 3.1 and scope 3.4 emissions for each supplier? and if so where can we get this insight? This was a question of one of our customers.

Thanks in advance for your feedback 

#freighttransportintegration

entry.title: Re: How SAP’s Generative AI Hub facilitates embedded, trustworthy, and reliable AI
entry.content: Very well crafted blog. Looking forward to the next updates.

entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to customise Notification message on new starter. The original notification message is as below. Could you pls guide on where to find the source of this original message?

"Congratulations! You have new direct report: xxxxx with effective date xxxxx. Pls work with xxxx to create objectives."

entry.title: Re: Enter a Reference for NF in PO tab or Material tab error
entry.content: Forwarded to Globalization Services

entry.title: Re: Sloc to sloc against the bin to bin transfer
entry.content: We dont want change any destination any physical destination bin, Once the Physical desination bin is given will be the same still the TO confirmation. But in this case i could not find any field entry to enter the physical destination bin.

entry.title: Re: In CIDS How do you MERGE 2 INPUT files into 1 OUTPUT File
entry.content: 1. Create a field "Join_Field_1" in first table MARD and hardcode the value as '1'.

entry.title: Collective Billing of Solution Order:Include Prof.Serv. Project and Other Docs into single invoice
entry.content:
Solution Business Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud enables you to sell bundled solutions combining products, services, subscriptions, long-term services, and projects to become a one-stopshop to solve complete customer problems [Ref : Selling Business Outcomes with Bundled Solutions].

As part of this scenario, it can make sense to create a single invoice per solution order.

In previous releases, it was not possible to converge billing data from customer projects (that are part of the same solution order) into a combined invoice with billing data from other documents that are also part of the solution order, such as service orders, service contract, and so on. [Ref: One Invoice Per Solution Order]. Customer projects had to be invoiced separately.

As of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408, you can use billing type CBD2 to combine all billing-relevant follow-on documents of a solution order into a single invoice. Refer to note 3413050 for an example of how to create combined invoice of the solution order document.

entry.title: Re: Unable to generate/open docx - SAP Enable Now
entry.content: Thank you Shane, the WordTest.exe worked great!

entry.title: Re: Task 8 - Responding to an OData query with navigation (July Developer Challenge - "Reverse
entry.content:
Hi @MioYasutake , bang on! That was the problem, a mere typo. 🙂 Thanks for your help!

@qmacro : Please find below my submission for Task 8.

 

Tried few OData calls and its in line with the regular OData calls.
